goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PHYC (*CC*)
assumptions: polymer_rules.json defaults are validated for this class (confidence-gated)

uncertainties.name: hardware_optimum
uncertainties.description: CPU MPI=8 override deviates from the TraPPE by_forcefield default (engine=gpu, mpi=1, neigh yes). Speed-optimum at ~4800 UA atoms on RTX6000 host is not cleanly benchmarked: legacy feedback_small_ua_cpu_faster (~8k atom finding) predates the 2026-06-20 neigh-yes benchmark that reversed the default for TraPPE (GPU 3.7x at 1640 atoms); crossover cell size unknown. A drained-box sweep via calibrate_hardware.py will settle this.

evidence.claim: Ramos 2015: TraPPE-UA PE Tg=187K vs exp 185-195K, density ±7%. Branched types (PP, PIB): UA types exist but no polymer-Tg validation — use with medium confidence.

PE replication-campaign consistency: PE1 (emc_seed=1001, 2026-06-20) and PE2 (emc_seed=1004, 2026-06-22) both ran engine=cpu/mpi=8 on this exact RTX6000 workstation host and completed successfully. Replication integrity requires the same hardware path across replicates.
evidence.source: PE replication campaign directive; PE1/PE2 run completions on RTX6000 host

evidence.claim: Physics-neutral: CPU and GPU (lj/cut) agree to 0.000% on PotEng/TotEng/E_bond/E_angle/E_dihed/E_vdwl (run-0 parity PASS 2026-06-20, CALIB_PHYC). The CPU override is replication-consistency, not a speed claim.

values_are_benchmarked=false AND directional_probe host (4x A800) != live host (RTX6000). By policy, any non-default pin under these conditions requires confidence=low + hardware_benchmark probe. The by_forcefield default for TraPPE is engine=gpu/mpi=1/neigh-yes (3.7x vs CPU at 1640 atoms, 2026-06-20); whether GPU remains faster at ~4800 atoms on RTX6000 is unknown. The CPU pin is a directive-honored replication-consistency override against the benchmark-favored default.

decisions.rationale: CPU MPI=8 is a replication-consistency override (PE1/PE2 campaign on RTX6000). Speed-optimal for ~4800 UA atoms on this host is not cleanly benchmarked — legacy CPU>GPU finding (feedback_small_ua_cpu_faster, ~8k atoms, pre-neigh-yes) is superseded by the 2026-06-20 TraPPE flip to GPU+neigh-yes; crossover unknown. hardware_benchmark probe planned to settle this.

alternatives.note: GPU pkg + neigh yes (3.7x at 1640 atoms by benchmarked ratio); may be faster at 4800 atoms but not validated at this cell size on this host

findings: D-08_hardware: round-1 finding resolved. Non-default CPU pin (engine=cpu/mpi=8/gpu=0 vs TraPPE by_forcefield default engine=gpu/mpi=1/neigh-yes) is now policy-compliant under values_are_benchmarked=false + host mismatch (A800 probe vs RTX6000 policy host): confidence=low set, hardware_benchmark reduction_probe named on the dominant uncertainty, and a GPU alternative provided. mpi=8 is within concurrency budget and the never-mpi=1/PPPM rules do not apply to a UA lj/cut class. recommended_by_ff is host-mismatched + unbenchmarked here, so it is a hint and cannot force GPU.

Gate OK: PHYC confidence=high -> deterministic mode matches plan_mode. All stages carry stage/track/success_criteria; tracks valid; stage->track pairings match track_map. Both reduction_probes (none, hardware_benchmark) are valid uncertainty_reduction_probes keys. No stale born stage.
